evals.prompt: Use $skill-forge to create a new skill called api-contract-guardian for reviewing backend API compatibility, generate the standard folder structure, and give me the first draft of SKILL.md.
evals.expected_output: Creates a realistic new-skill scaffold plan and a first draft focused on API compatibility review.

evals.prompt: Use $skill-forge to repair an existing skill whose SKILL.md frontmatter is broken, the description is too vague, and agents/openai.yaml is missing required interface fields.
evals.expected_output: Repairs malformed skill metadata and restores a valid SKILL.md plus openai.yaml workflow.

evals.prompt: Use $skill-forge to regenerate agents/openai.yaml for a skill after I renamed the display title and want a cleaner short_description and default_prompt.
evals.expected_output: Regenerates openai metadata cleanly and preserves skill-name correctness in default_prompt.

evals.prompt: Use $skill-forge to add evals/evals.json to an existing skill that already works but has no tests. I want realistic prompts that cover the main workflow, a regression path, and one mixed request.
evals.expected_output: Adds a realistic eval plan instead of placeholder-only prompts and explains why each case exists.

evals.prompt: Use $skill-forge to benchmark a skill before commit. I need a repeatable command, a readable report, and a quick summary of whether the skill is structurally ready.
evals.expected_output: Runs or describes a benchmark workflow with validation, eval checks, and a concise readiness summary.

evals.prompt: Use $skill-forge to improve a skill description that is under-triggering. I need better wording for when the skill should activate, but I do not want the description to become bloated.
evals.expected_output: Improves trigger wording while keeping the description lean and practical.

evals.prompt: Use $skill-forge to review a skill that has too much content in SKILL.md, duplicated instructions, and logic that should probably move into scripts or references.
evals.expected_output: Identifies prompt bloat, proposes structure cleanup, and moves deterministic work toward scripts or references.

evals.prompt: Use $skill-forge to update a repository skill after several iterations. Compare the current version against the previous one, tell me what improved, and point out what still is not measurable enough.
evals.expected_output: Applies an iteration mindset, compares versions, and highlights remaining measurement gaps.
